item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm at loss for words. All I can say that this could make a great movie. Thank you for writing this authors.

+1

+400

Congrats! It deserves every one of those updoots!

To all past and future ones!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great Tale, but the SCP in the article is the Pit, which is neither developed enough to be interesting nor integral to the excellent tale. As a mainlist article, it's a -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea that the pit is being used to reboot SCPs is very cool but not enough attention is paid to the concept (my favorite bits are the tables first listing the mystery corpses and then listing SCPs that have been disposed of) and then it just turns into an action novel without going back to the pit. Feels like it would be better if the story part of it were a separate tale as the SCP just acts as a background element.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is easily my favorite 5k entry - it’s phenomenal. Though I think it fits into the 001 slot a bit more that it does into one from the “main list”, it doesn’t detract from its incredible quality.

Probably the easiest +1 in my life.

Great work!
Gives me some Ouroborous Cycle vibes. Love it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was indeed a hell of a read, but not the bad kind in the slightest. The twist at the end is simply beautiful and it makes me wish for an SCP-inspired board game LMFAO

Take my +1 kind sir

I also wish I could upload twice due to the [DATA EXPUNGED] reference in the [REDACTED]

item.title: Re: Welcome to the True Man's World
item.content:encoded: he fact this wasn't a fifth church articul is very hart braking. this should have been a 001 articul

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They do have a dark one, the dunkel which kinda looks like a stout. I like their standard wheat beer but the blue one that you mention is also pretty popular for whatever reason!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure what I was thinking when I said stout — changed it to Alkoholfrei — but I definitely remember thinking that my own old man had a bizarre fondness for their alcohol-free beers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure what you have against Erdinger and it definitely isn’t a stout, but this was an entertaining read all the same!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how the rating is just at the bottom cuz you know this SCP was badass.

I'm thrilled to see Made in Heaven continue its climb there! I like to think that it's made people less afraid of combining tales and GoI formats into their SCPs and more excited about innovative storytelling!

item.title: Re: ...Nobody? Really?
item.content:encoded:
Nah. Guy with 0 karma here but can't help with agreeing with you. The story and SCP were exceptionally entertaining to read so I gave it a +1 however, part of me can't help but be a little bothered by the "all powerful entity" and "world changing" cliches. Without the epilogue, I think a lot of people including me would be left confused by the ending. That's not unreasonable as many SCP's are left not entirely explained on purpose which adds another layer of fun while reading, but I suppose considering they were the one who wrote it, the author can do whatever they want to with their story.

While some people don't like concepts like these as much, it seems like there's an equal number who do so I suppose it's a matter of opinion.

item.title: The first lengthy SCP article that I bothered to read the entirety of
item.content:encoded:
Which is a testament to how interesting I found this skip. Admittedly, was impressed with the smooth ass formatting which was why it piqued my interest initially. Progressing through the parts only made me want to read more and as someone who isn't too well versed in deep side of SCP lore, it didn't take too much to find a basic understanding of the narrative. Also liked how there was a different style of information relay for the different characters. Enjoyed how it wasn't all serious as well. The lines "Jason jumped in the moment we told him he'd die for good. He seemed glad to go," and "You absolute goddamn bastard. I wish you were my real father so I could disown you." made me laugh out loud. Gonna have to write the last one down for future reference.

Took me a little under an hour to finish it all so good job making a enrapturing story author. +1

item.title: ...Nobody? Really?
item.content:encoded:
I loved every part of this SCP. The story's planned well, the JoJo reference is great, the writing is exceptional, and every part of this SCP made it better and better. Unfortunately, there are 8 parts. and an epilogue.

..Nobody. I- Ok I'll be real here, I'm getting a tad tired of every single article featuring the dude (with a few notable exceptions) having him be basically god controlling everything or this unstoppable force with no end in sight. Why can't he just be a small POI? Why can't the man of mystery remain a mystery. There's no mystery if the answer to every question is "yes."

This is in general a rant against how every single GOI and POI start out small and end up essentially controlling the world and running everything from the shadows. There is no canon, I know I know, but like… why can't things just stay small and compact sometimes? I don't know, I'm just ranting at this point.

The article was exceptional, but the epilogue threw a wrench right into my nutsack so -1.

I am fully expecting to be dunked on mercilessly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't "Caucasian" usually used instead?

Per Wikipedia: "This usage is considered erroneous by anthropologists and other scientists, who note that it conflates an anthropologically valid category (Caucasoid) with the social construct of the "white race". The conflation of Caucasian with white is also demographically misleading since the category Caucasoid has sometimes been considered to include various populations, such as South Asians and North Africans, that are not considered white in a social sense.[11]"

Thanks for pointing out the other issues though. I'll fix those shortly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an extraordinarily fun read. Mystery, murder, tying in all these GoIs and PoIs. I loved seeing the personality of each character shine through, even the ones that were mentioned just once.

However, this does feel much more like a tale than a SCP - a very good tale at that, but not a true SCP. No-voting because I love this, but the SCP aspect seems too much in the background and not tied into the ending.

A few typos/inconsistencies
– hide block

> A white teenage girl whose eyes have been gouged out.

Isn't "Caucasian" usually used instead?

Be ready to exfiltrate any time and anywhere

Extra space in "any time".

so Fritz will be probably be wise to those

Extra "be".

MANN:I knew Fritz

Missing space after colon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a big wall of WHAT! and HUH! but it makes sense in the end and is a solid scip though i do wish that 5555 went to a fifthisim scip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this! It tells an interesting story (although SoullessSingularity does make valid points in terms of extraneous parts) although I am somewhat disappointed that the 5555 slot isn't at all fifthist related, this is very good nonetheless.

item.title: Re: Very well done
item.content:encoded: Well hey, look on the bright side: When Series LVI opens up, they'll still have a chance at slot 55555.

item.title: Re: Welcome to the True Man's World
item.content:encoded:
The index also conveniently has 8 parts.
Coincidence? I think not.

item.title: Very well done
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the file and have no real complaints but the fact that this wasn't a fifthist file is a crime in one dimension or another

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It absolutely should not have. This article earned this slot by virtue of its placement in the 5000 contest, and whether or not it's about Fifthism has absolutely no factor into it.

I would certainly hope nobody would be so foolish as to downvote this article because they think something else should be in that slot, because that is textbook malicious downvoting as described in the site rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 5555 slot should have been reserved for a fifthist entry :(
As the contest reaches its end, it's nice to know that Made in Heaven secured the double-oh-oh! Here's to a new series of SCPs and a whole new wave of innovation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this tied to some sort of canon or something? I feel like I’m out of the loop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit too chaotic for the 5000 slot, but would work perfectly as a 001 proposal.

item.title: Re: Welcome to the True Man's World
item.content:encoded: I havent even read the article yet but youget my upvote because it looks like your refrencing Part 6 and 7 of JJBA, and I fucking respect that shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://jojowiki.com/Made_in_Heaven
https://jojowiki.com/Mandom

Combine the two, and—-Voalá!!! You've got the gist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
not a fan of evil Holly, but a fan of the post nonetheless

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone give me a spoiler summary?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this, but how the players were characterized is just so far off from how they've been characterized previously that this just feels foreign to the site as a whole. I know there's no canon, but there is some rhyme and reason to how commonly used characters (like GOI heads) are portrayed which it feels like this article just ignores. It's not even flying in the face of past characterizations, per se, because that would imply an intentional subversion which I don't think is really present. Subversion would but "What if the Foundation but _?" and this is more like "What if _?" which happens to use the aesthetics of the Foundation.

Like, it's really quite good and fun and I like it, but as far as I can tell it doesn't fit easily into the rest of the body of work which makes up (various visions of) the Foundation-verse.

item.title: Re: Spelling error?
item.content:encoded: Berth is a word that means "a place to sit or sleep especially on a ship or vehicle".

item.title: Spelling error?
item.content:encoded:
Dunno if this is intentional, or a mistake, but while i was reading Part 4 i found this quote:

"I was in a private berth so I shot out the window."

Did you mean booth?

Other than that, great story. Gripping characters, love the references to all the different lore, and incorporation of all canons (to a degree).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, although i agree with others that this makes more sense as a tale than a scip, i quite like it, and I think it's a nice look into an alternate universe, kind of like the why entry.

item.title: Jojo
item.content:encoded: This whole post is a jojo reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I downvoted until the very end when all the roles got switched.

I'm going to headcanon Marcell Duchamp and Jude Kriyot as the same game character archetype, even though Acidverse is completely incompatible with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup. This is it. This is the 5000 I was waiting for this whole contest. The scale, the sense of urgency, the escalation, the fresh take on old characters… everything was exactly what I had hoped for. This reads like the best 001 proposals and finishes like the best establishing tales for the best canons. +1 for sheer excellence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+Spoilery opinions
– hide block

Gotta agree with most of these criticisms. The overall concept, while certainly interesting and amusing to think about in its own right, was presented in such a way that the entire setting felt disjointed and unclear, and the lack of any real explanation or hints as to how the world got to the state that it's in (as well as the lack of any and all indicators as to how deep the conspiracy actually goes) ended up straining my suspension of disbelief to the point where I just couldn't immerse myself in the story being presented. There were also some smaller things that I took issue with, like Wondertainment being reduced to a standard crazy psycho lady and bluntfiend being one of the players in the "game" (I know that part of the premise is that the players switch roles each game and as a result GAW is more a front for the current leader than anything else, but it still bothers me immensely seeing GAW of all GoIs sidling up with the Foundation and participating in some callous authoritarian "game" no matter the context). I'm particularly frustrated by my issues with this article given that I was absolutely hooked by the initial set-up, with O5-1's far-too-optimistic memorandum and the wonderfully offputting and mysterious 5000 document setting me up for a darkly captivating thriller, only to end up reading what felt to me like an over-the-top spin-off Foundation action movie. I definitely feel like this concept could be written in such a way that I'd end up absolutely loving it, and as the article is now, I can't deny that it's well thought-out and deftly executed, but for me personally, it's not really doing all that much. No-voting.

for the record though the jojo reference was gr8

item.title: Re: Welcome to the True Man's World
item.content:encoded: I came just to comment that this should be a -001 entry. Then I saw the desired slots. It was meant to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, this was a fun read. Really enjoyed the mix of longer dialogues with different sorts of short snippets, to keep the piece from feeling like miles of scrolling. Liked that the details I had to guess at or solve for myself were not needlessly obscured nor revealed by a sledgehammer at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is not set in Broken Masquerade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Once I came to the part that says it's used to replace anomalies, I thought that second idea might have been what was going on. Granted, it seems you can also use it to replace anyone? I suppose an anomaly not being fully understood isn't something I can really complain about though. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can speak to a couple of your criticisms.

With regard to the middle section, Mann was previously writing his letters to his successor Overseer because he thought that Fritz was operating against the Foundation itself — the middle section reveals that Fritz has completely replaced everyone in the Foundation, leading Mann to determine to bring it down with Fritz.

As for the very first version of the 5000 document, there’s a couple ways to read it. I intended it to be a red herring that Fritz wrote as part of his ploy to frame Mann, but some readers have posited that the things Fritz wrote about actually existed, being previous incarnations of existing anomalies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everett Mann escaped the Cogwork Orthodoxy by dressing as a mime. I really do not even.

If the cat wants someone dead, that motherfucker is gonna be sleeping it off in a wooden kimono.

Obligatory complaint that this feels more like a tale than an article, but I'm not gonna lie. This was really fucking good, and in particular I love this line. Lots of great one-liners, to be honest.

I will say I got confused in the middle. After Fritz gets hold of Mann, it seemed like the latter was switching sides, got brainwashed or something, but then he switched back?

My other criticism is that I wish this had ended on a final iteration of the documentation. That's a real nail in the coffin of "this is actually a tale", you're not focused enough on it. And I actually still don't get the first one; it's like they forgot who all those objects originally were or something, and there's no explanation why. Still upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a fun read but the ending was confusing. Like I think I get what the author was implying, that all in the big goi leaders are bored immoral’s who use the world as a play thing (which I like) but the last part where they switch roles is the confusing(/bad?) part. Like do they go back in time and do it again but with the roles different, if so then that’s not clear at all. Also looking back scp 5000 is barely apart of the tale since you can read the article but skip over the scp itself and you wouldn’t lose much (which shouldn’t happen when writing a tale intertwined with a scp article).

But other than that great work +1

Also also the article is missing the broken masquerade tag

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember all the different versions of this I was shown back in the drafting phase, and I have to say I enjoyed the final product. As a mystery, I found it to be an entertaining romp, and the various exchanges between the GoI leaders were pretty funny (I especially enjoyed Jude's emails.).

Not really much else to say. Job well done you three. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. It’s a fun ride, it touches on a vast swathe of existing SCP lore while still being its own unique idea. It’s hectic and eclectic and I wouldn’t want it any other way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For me, it felt kinda "meh." I can respect the effort and the dialogue, but the concept is not something I would want as 5000. Put simply, this reads like a cheesy Foundation action movie titled "The Mann" starring Leonardo DiCaprio as our dashing old-yet-youthful protagonist. That isn't a bad thing, it was enjoyable to read, but that alone isn't what merits something to take the 5000 slot. Maybe it would be better as an 001 proposal? It certainly feels like one. In its own little world, it entirely changes the basis of everything the foundation is about and throws a twist at you that's revealed throughout the article, and anything that deals with the fundamental nature of the foundation so intensely makes the most sense to be an 001 in my eyes. I understand that others might have different perspectives on what makes something a good 001 proposal instead of just a lore-heavy article, of course.

Novote for now, probably an upvote after the contest and definitely an upvote if it's made into an 001.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lot of documents to throw at the reader for not much exciting stuff happening. Unfortunately, I got to the end and all I could think was "that's it?" for such a grand buildup. Then again, I've extremely rarely been one for articles trying to shoehorn in as many GoIs as possible.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Thank you to everyone who voted and commented on Made in Heaven thus far!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just about sums up my opinion as well. I read it a few times just to see if there was something I was missing. By the end, I felt just… nothing? Maybe annoyance. A lot of work clearly went into it but it just didn't really amount to anything I found interesting. It ultimately didn't really feel like I was reading an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately this felt very low-key to me. Mann is presented as impossibly badass, so he never feels particularly human or at-risk. Fritz is presented as always being one step (or maybe a hundred steps) ahead, so it also never felt much like Mann would achieve his objective. And many of the things that Mann does are only described by him, not shown, so I found it difficult to get fully engaged in his quest. I'd also echo the thought that making the whole thing a game, with no life-and-death stakes for the other players either (and having them all chat regularly about how the game was going), meant that I didn't feel the consequences of the many cameos and the violence inflicted on them - in the end, either the whole world are their playthings (in which case there are no stakes at all) or mundane humanity does have real agency (in which case the ancillary death toll is horrific and treated without any weight).

Finally, and I know this is out-of-step with many authors, my personal view is that the challenge of writing a skip is to create an epistolary story that is constructed of believably in-universe documents. Having done that for the vast majority of this, Part 8 ditches the conceit for straight prose, which feels to me a bit like an easy way out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, just wow. There are lots of SCPs that go full on tale but this one is just… just so good! I Neede a little bit of time to get infested but I got sucked in pretty fast. We gotta kill Fritz bois!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If nothing else, I'm glad that the ending hooked you! I worked pretty hard on their interactions and it's good that it paid off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though it's a well-written article, I feel like this is more of an action movie setup than a 5k entry. I had a hard time being captivated by it because I've read this same story time and time again. Still a fascinating idea, but I wish the SCP document was more of the focus rather than the notes.

Edit: That being said, I'd love to see a Tale about a world where Wondertainment started/is a part of/adapted the Broken God religion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a little embarrassed to admit it because of how this is being received, but I had such a hard time getting immersed in this article that it literally took me all day to read it. I kept getting distracted by other things. which is frustrating, I was hoping to spend all day reading 5k entries, this was the first one I opened, and my work schedule from tomorrow to Thursday is going to make it tough to finish as much as I'd have liked to. I know that's not the authors' fault, it's entirely my own. this didn't really grab me until the very end, the dialogue between mann and fritz. maybe I'm not smart enough to get it or appreciate it like it should be? I didn't have any specific issue with the quality of the writing here, it just didnt captivate me at all til almost the end. :-/ that said, I probably won't downvote bc I generally don't do that at all. I'm clearly in the minority, and that's not a bad thing. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It reads to me that you don't like how it interacts with yours.

item.title: Re: Welcome to the True Man's World
item.content:encoded: I noticed your jojo reference here :>
